preamble: The voiceless alveolar plosive /t/ can undergo palatalization, a sound change where its articulation shifts towards the hard palate, often resulting in a voiceless alveolo-palatal affricate /tɕ/. This transformation is typically triggered by the presence of a following high front vowel or palatal glide.
phoneticEffects: Palatalization, affrication, place assimilation (alveolar to alveolo-palatal)

examples.note: Palatalization of /t/ before front vowels, as seen in morphophonological alternations.

sources: Jang, H. (2026). A muscle-based articulatory and OT account of Japanese /t/-alternations. Linguistic Research, 43(1), 227-256.
